Introduction to Product Launch Formula
!
Welcome to the PLF Core training. I have three videos here for you in this Core
Training. The whole idea is to give you a jump start before we get into module one.
One of the key differences between Product Launch Formula and many trainings, and
trust me, I go through a lot of different trainings, is that it's proven. I've had success
after success, but I have hundreds and hundreds of clients that have had great
successes in just about every kind of niche and every kind of market that you can
imagine.
!
I started developing these strategies when I first started my very first online business in
1996. I spent about eight or nine years, from 1996 until 2005, using these strategies,
developing the strategies, tweaking the strategies - in a market that had nothing to do
with building your business, marketing, or entrepreneurship. I was in the stock market,
a completely different market.
!
Then in 2003, I started teaching a few friends about this, and I started to realize these
techniques worked in other markets besides in the stock market. Then in 2005, I
released the very first version of Product Launch Formula, and the proof's in the
pudding. We have hundreds of clients that together have done half a billion
dollars in sales.
!
It just crushes, and it just keeps on rolling along. It's been continually updated, I keep
on updating. I keep on adding in the latest strategies and tactics that I learn, many of
which my students bring to me. This is tested; it's proven. You're in the right place. This
will work for you.
!
One of the things I wanted to talk about was just the overall conceptual aspects of
Product Launch Formula. One of my successes early on was because I realized that the
online world, the Internet, was a great place to use a lot of the old-school direct-
marketing tactics and strategies - so even though I had no background in old-school
direct marketing, I studied it because that was the only thing you could study back in
1996. There was no one teaching how to build your online business, how to market
online, anything like that, so I went back old-school, and I started studying some of the
old-school masters. There's been stuff since the 1920s that you could study about old-
school direct marketing.
!
That's where my first leanings and education was. There are three core
fundamentals in direct marketing: the list, the offer, and the copy. Three big
sections in direct marketing.
!
The list is basically the list ... and back then it would be like direct mail usually, or you
might be advertising in a magazine or a newspaper. But a lot of times just direct mail, it
would be the list that you were mailing. A lot of times you would rent a list or buy a list
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_PLF Core v1.0
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 15 --

and send that direct mail. You would always have to worry about getting the right list
because if you had a list of people that were interested in fly-fishing, and you tried to
sell them something about playing guitar, you probably were not going to get great
results because some of those fly-fisher people are probably interested in guitar, but not
many of them, not all of them. You had to get the right list.
!
The offer is what you are actually offering them. What are you going to give them?
What do they get when they place an order with you, when they buy your stuff?
!
Then copy is the sales copy. Back then it was usually direct marketing, like sales letters.
It would be like printed out and sent to them, or part of an advertisement.
!
There was list, offer, and copy. The way it worked is, you could actually not get one of
these right (you could sort of screw up on one of these) and still be OK. If you had the
right list and the right offer, but you didn't have great copy, you'd still be OK. Or if you
had a lousy offer, but you had the right list, and you had great copy, you'd probably be
OK.
!
You might get a lot of returns, but you'd probably make a lot of sales. Then if you didn't
really have a list, or if you just had a general list and it wasn't very targeted, but you
had an incredible offer and an incredible copy, then you'd be OK.
!
To give an example, Gary Halbert, a legendary direct-marketing copywriter, passed
away several years ago. I actually got to meet Gary on a few occasions and go out to
dinner with him once. That was really cool, he’s a brilliant genius. He had a great
example: if you had a hamburger stand, what is the one thing that you would want
more than anything else in your marketing?
!
He said, "Well, you have a hamburger stand, but you could take all the copy in the
world, and throw it away. If you had a crowd of starving people, it doesn't matter. It
doesn't really matter how good your food was. It doesn't matter how you advertised it,
what the copy was. As long as you had a starving audience you're going to sell a lot of
hamburgers." Right?
!
You have the right offer at the right time. On the other hand, if you have some people
that are just mildly hungry, but you have a fantastic offer (as in you have free
hamburgers, and you advertise it in a really great way) you’re probably going to be able
to either give away or sell a bunch of food, OK?
!
The final one was if you had hungry people, and you had a bunch of burgers, you
probably don't need good copy. You can see how you can drop one of those three out
of there, and you'd still be OK. Now, if you get all three of them working, then
you win. That's when you're going to have this massive, massive success.
!
That's old-school marketing. Now, the interesting thing in our world with Product
Launch Formula is, we've changed this around a little bit because you don't really need

!
In terms of the offer, because of the launch conversation you're going to have (which
you’ll learn inside Product Launch Formula), as you release pre-launch content the
market is going to tell you what your offer should be. That's the beautiful thing.
!
Because of the sequential nature of your launch, you're not depending on one great
sales letter, so you're able to fine-tune your copy as you go through your launch. These
three things almost build themselves for you as you go through your launch, which is a
beautiful thing.
!

!
These continually expand as you stack your launches, as you go from one launch to

!
Now, down the road, you might put together a great launch, and you get into
Evergreen. You can start buying traffic and really building up an autopilot launch
business here. Initially just through word of mouth, you're going to build up these inner
two circles. That's sort of, in a nutshell, Product Launch Formula.
!
Now, there are a few elephants in the room. Let's just run through them. List and

PLF Road Map
!
OK so now I just want to cover the what of Product Launch Formula, just a quick
roadmap of the course and what you'll learn and where within Product Launch Formula.
This is a big program because there's only one way I know how to teach, and that's to
give you everything. It is a lot, there's a lot here but just remember, one of my favorite
things -- you're going to hear me say this a lot -- it's all about the baby steps. It's about
taking steps, moving forward in the direction you're going to. No one jumps to a million
dollar launch overnight, it just doesn't happen. You start off with small successes and
you build them.
It's just like a baby, they take that first step and they fall down, but they get up and try
to take another attempt, and they fall down. But eventually they put a couple steps
together and sooner or later they're walking. Every baby learns to walk and if you just
take the baby steps and just keep moving forward in this program, you'll have that
same experience. It will work for you.
You don't need to do it all, you don't need to do every single type of launch, you don't
have to do every single piece. The model is so powerful that you can get 20% of it right
and you're still going to win. You don't have to get it all right or all perfect, I make
mistakes in every single launch I do. But it doesn't matter because the model is so
redundant, and so powerful, and so strong, that it's going to end up working for you.
Let's walk through the modules. Module 1 is basically the core strategies and
tactics. What I cover in here is what makes the whole thing tick, what makes Product
Launch Formula work. If you only go through this one module, then you win and you
win forever. You will change your results forever. You'll need to do the core work in here
and I've got some actual exercises in here but you will use them in all of your launches
going forward. So the core work that you're doing in module 1 works for all your
launches, no matter whether they're seed launches or JV launches or quick launches or
internal launch, doesn't matter. The core work is going to continue to work for you as
you move forward.
And the core exercises, you might change businesses, you might radically change your
business model, but you can still go back to the work or exercise that you did in
module. Go through it all again and you're going to be set up for a launch all over
again. This is where you learn to connect with your market to create great offers and a
great pre-launch, and really develop the core ingredients of your launch. So module
one, that sounded pretty cool right? Pretty big right?
Module 2 we take it to the next step. We take the work you did in module 1
and we start to develop your launch theme and your launch story. We'll go
deep into what I call the PS Path, which is how you deliver huge value and still make
the sale. It's how you avoid giving away the farm, giving away everything and giving

away just the right amount of money, right amount of content so it leads into the sale

Now the seed launch is great if you have no list and no product. It's a
beautiful thing because you basically, you get paid to create your product.
I love it. That's how I did my first launch for when I first rolled out Product
Launch Formula. When I first started teaching product launches, I did it as a seed
launch. I started off with six sales and it grew up into this huge business that I've got
today, it's been amazing. The quick launch is great if you've already got a
business and an offer, but you just want a quick pop of money. This is the
easiest and quickest launch - that's the quick launch. I did it once to pay my taxes, I did
it once to finish my basement - it’s a great way to bring in money quick.
One thing I forgot to mention about that seed launch is it's great to get started if you
have no list and no product. But say you already have an existing business and you
want to come out with a new product, but you just can't seem to get yourself to put in
the time to create the product - it’s great for that too.
Now module 4, the big focus of module 4 is on the internal launch. The internal launch
is basically the classic sideways sales letter launch with the three pieces of content, plus
the sales letter/video, but it's just a launch to your existing list. I love internal launches,
they're the most elegant launches in my opinion. They're fun to do, you have complete
control over every aspect, it's fantastic. It's a logical next step after you do the seed
launch to go to do an internal launch. We also go deep into creating prelaunch content
in module 4 and the great thing about this module is this where we start to get into
really high leverage because those internal launches are high leverage launches.
And also I'll go deep into some of the launch list, how you create a launch list,
how you manage the launch list - we’ll go deep into launch list secrets in module 4.
Now module 5, which is the best module ever. I love this module because this is where
I go deep into breaking down prelaunch content videos. This is probably the best
teaching I have ever done. I'll actually walk you through prelaunch videos from
some of my students and you get to actually see their videos, and then I'll stop the
videos and I say, "Oh look at what they did here, this is awesome. This is really great,
this is where you - look at how he established authority here."
"Look at this is reciprocity here, look how he did this." "Oh he missed this, this is
where he screwed up here," it's fantastic. It’s just real world stuff. You're going to love

created that internal launch or that JV launch, then you can build a system so that
every time a new lead comes into your business, they end up going through your
evergreen launch.
Basically you have the power of PLF, but you have it going all year round for you

I think about John Gallagher, my prototypical case study. The guy was on food
stamps, and he had two young children at home he was trying to support. He
was going to school to be an acupuncturist. He was working at a non-profit. He could
barely make ends meet for his wife and his two kids.
!
One day he came home and designed a board game. It was about edible and medicinal
plants and herbs. It was an educational board game for kids about how to go in your
backyard and pick plants and use them for food or medicine. He tried to roll out that
first.
!
He had a small newsletter list that he built with borrowed wi-fi and he had a borrowed
laptop. He built up this little tiny newsletter list and he tried to sell his board game to
them and he sold 12 games. Then he got Product Launch Formula, put together a
proper launch and sold 670 games. So from 12 to 670 is pretty amazing. Now he's
sold 50,000 games.
!
That was just the tip of the iceberg because then he sold a physical product. It was a
kit for making herbal things, tinctures and whatever like that. So it was an herbal kit.
Every month he did a launch for that. Then he came out with a membership site. That
membership site was 10 bucks a month, but he's got thousands and thousands of
people on that membership site now. He does 1 launch a year for the membership site
and would put in 4 or 500 people into that membership site. 500 people is 5000 dollars
a month. He would put those in and those people would stick because they love him
because of this pre-launch content.
!
Then eventually he started to come out with courses. Online courses about the herbal
world, about all kinds of different areas within that whole herbal, edible, medicinal
plants and herbs market. The thing is, John's not a guru. He's knowledgeable about his
topic, but he's not the guru. His knowledge is bringing the guru's to the market because
all these gurus who know how to treat colds or help you physically, give you more
energy or whatever with herbs and with plants, but they don't know how to market.
He's got the marketing skills and he brings them to the market. He's not the guru.
There are a lot of people that follow that path. Incredibly powerful and John has built
up this great business now.
!
On the other hand there's Susan Garrett. Susan Garrett teaches about dog agility. She's
a dog agility trainer and dog agility is this little niche within dog training, which is a
niche within the dog market, which is a niche within the pet market. Dog agility is a
pretty small little niche. It's a competitive sport and Susan is very, very accomplished
and she's a great trainer.
!
She competes too but she would also help other people on how to train their dogs. She
could only work with a few people per year. She actually got Product Launch Formula
with a goal to, “make my money back before the credit card bill comes in and my
husband can see it”. She did just that.
!

Again, online products. She's done launch after launch and she's grown her business

expert when it comes to the dog agility training market. There is no one that has a
business anything like hers, that gets the results that she gets, helps as many people as
she does. She's built a great business. She was a great trainer, but she wasn't
necessarily seen as the top guru in terms of the training. Now she is.
!
Then there's me. When I started I was Mr. Mom, home taking care of a couple kids, a
couple little babies. I started teaching about the stock market, which was crazy because
I didn't have experience. I wasn't a stock broker. I wasn't a registered investment
advisor. I didn't have any of those things going on. I didn't have any credentials at all, I
just started publishing. But I became known as the expert. The interesting thing was
that there was someone in the background who had far more expertise than me, and I
was publishing a lot of his work. We were partners, so I was publishing his work. He
was in the background and I was the front man. As far as the world knew, I was the
expert, but I really had someone in the background.
!
So you can see there's different ways to go at this. John Gallagher, he brings the gurus
to the market. In a way, I brought the guru to the market, but he didn't want his name
out there, he wanted my name out there. So it's just a different way to do it. I built up
a very nice business that ran that for 8 or 9 years.
!
Now I’m Jeff version 2.0, with Product Launch Formula. Again I'm set up as the expert
or the authority or the guru, whatever word you want to use. I'm teaching my own
stuff. I started off with a seed launch. I sold 6. The first time, it was a Product
Launch workshop. I sold 6 of them. Now I've got over 10,000 students.
!
I've gone on to be a #1 New York Times best selling author. I get asked to speak all
over the place. So my expertise has been built up just by doing launches. People see
me do my launches and now I'm top of the heap there. In fact, now I get asked to
speak all the time, not just on marketing or business - other things too, even personal
development.
!
Then there's Greg. Greg is probably the most successful PLF Owner there is period.
Greg is sort of a similar, he’s not the front man. He's not the guru. In their business,
back when he first got Product Launch Formula way back in 2006, he was struggling
just to make this business stay profitable. He took PLF and he just ran with it. He just
ran and ran and ran.
!
He keeps on getting better at it and he's now done over 50 million dollars.
Again, he's not the front person, he's not the expert. He came in with a struggling

business, and now over the last 10 years he’s done 50 million dollars in launches, plus a
lot more going on.
!
So there's lots of different paths through Product Launch Formula. All those people I
talked about, none of them are teaching internet marketing or how to make money
online. There’s all different kinds of niches. The thing that all of them have in common
is that they made incremental change. It's all about doing the one launch and getting
better. Launch stacking. The results stacking where you just keep on getting better and
better and better. Taking baby steps and moving forward.
!
So there's many paths, and you don't know what your ultimate path is going to be. The
thing is, Product Launch Formula is almost like a super power. It gives you this ability to
create this amazing promotion at will whenever you want. As you're going to learn, it's
not just a promotion for products. It shows you how to influence people. How to get
them to take action. That action could be making the sale. It could be getting them to
buy your product. It could be getting them to join your list. It could be getting them to
call you up for an appointment. To attend your seminar. To buy your book. To watch
your video. No matter what, Product Launch Formula is going to get you there.
!
It might sound crazy, but it's great for products and I'm going to teach you that too. I'm
going to give you 100% exactly what you need to launch your products, but really it's
more - it’s about you. It's about you becoming a center of influence, becoming
attractive. I'm not saying attractive like physically attractive. I'm just saying where
people are naturally attracted to you, they want to be around you. They want to be part
of your world. It makes you more charismatic, more powerful.
!
Once you have those things, you can have anything you want. The business side
becomes really easy. For me, business is really easy now. It's not hard. It's not hard for
me to make sales. It's not hard for me to grow my business. It's not hard for me to
attract an amazing team. When you become attractive, all those things become really,
really powerful.
!
Now the thing is, you can just make money, or you can save the world. You can make a
huge impact on the world. Most of my PLF owners, they start off just wanting to make
money, but they evolve towards a greater sense of purpose. There's this
“entrepreneur's hierarchy of needs”. You've probably heard of “Maslow's hierarchy of
needs”, this is similar.
!
I've been thinking about this for a long time. My business coach Dan Sullivan has
helped me think about this as well. When you start out, it's usually about money only.
Then, for some people there's a turnaround. It usually starts off, you just want to make
some money. You just have to pay some bills, build a lifestyle, support your family.
!
Then when you got that money part taken care of, then it's about “I need some time”.
Sometimes it's flipped around. People come in, they've already got money, they just
need some time. Sometimes these flip around, but basic value of time and money.

-- 13 of 15 --

Freedom of money, freedom from want, freedom from time to do what you want. Then
when you get up in here, when you get your time and money figured out, then it
becomes relationship. Who are you with? Who are you working with? Who are your
clients? Who do you get to help? Who do you get to serve?
!
For me, one big thing is that we don't suffer bad clients. We fire clients. If we have a
client and they're not treating my support staff the way I want them to, we'll say thanks
and here's your money back and have a great life. We just don't deal with people like
that. There’s just some people in the world that are hard to work with and we don't
deal with them. I suggest you do the same thing. There's nothing more empowering
than firing clients. If you don't know that yet, you will learn it soon.
!
After you get the money and the time figured out, then it's about relationship. Then
once you get all those in place, then it does become (generally for most people) about
purpose. One of the words I love is impact. For me, I've got a pretty simple
lifestyle. The business does really well and I've got this great income. You're never
going to hear me say I've got too much money because that would just be bad mental
programming and bad energy to put out in the universe. I'm doing OK. It's not like I
have to do another launch to make more money. I have to do another launch to make a
greater impact. To help more entrepreneurs, because those entrepreneurs go out and
they help so many other people, so it ripples.
!
I help people, and generally the people I help are the kind that are out there helping
more people. Those people out there in that third ring, they're just happier, they're
achieving more, they're feeling better and they got better relationships. They impact so
many more people further out. It's wonderful and that's what it is about for me, it's
purpose. I see this happen for so many of my PLF owners. They go up this hierarchy of
needs. They go from money, to time to relationship to eventually purpose. You could
have all of those if you just take this stuff and you learn it and just embody it.
!
That's Product Launch Formula for you. It's been a crazy ride for me, its going to be a
crazy ride for you. The thing is, like I said earlier, you don't know what your path is yet.
I didn’t even know what my path was. When I was teaching about the stock market, I
thought that was going to be it. Then I started teaching PLF and I thought I was just
going to teach a few launches, but now I'm teaching people how to change the world. I
don't know what my end game is, but it's been a great ride and I know it's going to be
a similar ride for you.
!
Welcome aboard. It's going to a lot of fun. It's going to be amazing for you. We’ve now
